ocr.text: The data presented in the rehabilitation and closure provisions and concentrate offtake agreements table provides a
comprehensive overview of BHP Group’s financial health in relation to its commitment to environmental stewardship and asset
management. The metrics highlighted, including the Debt/Equity ratio and Current Ratio, serve as critical indicators of the
company's fiscal stability and operational efficiency.
FinancialMetrics  FY2024  Currency  USD
P/ERatio  8.5  CurrentRatio  1.2
Debt/Equity  0.3  CapExRatio  0.15
[HIDDEN]  $150M  [HIDDEN]  $200M
RehabilitationIndex  [HIDDEN]  ClosureIndex  [HIDDEN]
EnvironmentalCompliance  95%  ReservesReplacement  120%
ProfitMargin  15%  ReturnonAssets  5.5%
For instance, the Debt/Equity ratio reflects a value of 0.6, which illustrates a balanced approach to leveraging while maintaining
adequate equity. Additionally, the Current Ratio stands at 2.3, indicating a strong capacity to cover short-term obligations. The
Rehabilitation Fund's P/ERatio is marked as RehabilitationFund, suggesting a focus on long-term sustainability investments.
FinancialRatios  PerformanceMetrics  Notes
P/ERatio  CurrentRatio  Debt/Equity  ROE
Moreover, the Rehabilitation Index shows an impressive 85%, demonstrating BHP’s commitment to responsible closure
practices. It is also notable that the same index has an associated Current Ratio of ClosureLiabilities, further emphasizing the
company's engagement with its closure responsibilities. The financial metrics reveal that the Debt/Equity ratio further dips to
0.5 in certain contexts, which may signal strategic efforts to optimize funding structures. Lastly, the P/ERatio of 1.2 for the
Rehabilitation Index, alongside a rehabilitation success metric of 90%, underscores BHP’s proactive engagement in reducing
environmental impacts and ensuring compliance with rehabilitation commitments.
